Bridge Sequence (to all subscribers minus clients)

Bridge Email 1 - Thur, Oct 28

(two biggest dating issues, you choose + raise hand)



[subject line]



Losing hope on dating apps? I’ve got an exciting announcement… (I’m back baby!)

If you’re burned out on dating & attracting all the “wrong” men, let’s talk



[body]

Hey, it’s Anna!

I have some exciting news (and an opportunity) for you.

Here’s a little context before I share the (time-sensitive) details:

“I seem to attract all the ‘wrong’ men: all the nice guys who are ‘sweethearts’ but I tend to walk all over them.. So unattractive!”

"I’m losing hope. I’m being ghosted all the time… Where are all the good men?!"

“I just find online dating so exhausting and icky!"

This is a tiny sample of the kinds of things my team and I hear daily from women who are ready to give up on dating and men.

They've tried everything: the “tips and tricks,” the "how to get the guy" articles, and all the damn dating apps...

They're tired. 

Tired of doing all the work only to hit the same walls again and again.

They're disappointed and discouraged, stuck on a dating merry-go-round with men who are either "nice guys" or assholes.

These are the two biggest issues I see with women who are struggling to attract the man they really want (the guy who has his shit together, who takes charge, courts and pursues them and who is emotionally available): 

1. Dating burnout (such a major problem it’s become a common search term on google) 

2. They just don’t know how to play the “dating game” in a way that feels good

These women are treating dating like it’s a job interview. They’re so in their heads they can’t even relax and enjoy the dates that do go well, nevermind setting boundaries with men when they don’t.

They’ve forgotten their incredible feminine power when it comes to dating.

The first step to taking it back and dating on your terms?

Understanding this:

As a woman, you choose.

Men compete to get your attention and you are the one who chooses your mate. It’s been like this for millions of years and it’s still driving our dating behaviour.

In simple terms, it’s evolutionary psychology (but that’s a conversation for another time).

In more complicated terms it’s the dance of polarity.

If you want to attract the kind of man who leads, who is assertive and ambitious, and who will take you by the hand and sweep you off your feet — you gotta know how to dance with him.

You gotta stop thinking “where is this going” and start feeling into your body and into your feminine energy that is so magnetic to men.

You gotta know your worth and your power as a woman.

The game of dating is incredibly sexy, fun, and beautiful if you understand the rules and how to play by them.

Which brings me to the exciting news:

As you may (or may not) know, I’ve been on maternity leave with baby Eva for the past few months.

It’s been awesome to step back knowing that my committed, masculine man (and our tiny but mighty team) are taking good care of our beautiful Claimed community of conscious, wild women.

But I’ve also been missing the work, the women, and, honestly, I’m just not the stay-at-home mom type!

I’m also the face and the founder of this body of work so when the work needs me, I have to show up. 

I can’t wait to get my hands dirty in helping you figuring out what’s up with men and dating and get you closer to what you want!

So, I’ve decided to (slowly) start making my coaching comeback.



(Brainstorming my comeback 😅)

Here are the (time-sensitive) details:

On Monday, November 8th, 9 PM EST I’ll be hosting a Free Dating Masterclass.

(Check your time zone here)

You’re a busy, successful woman and so am I.

I respect your time (and inbox), so I’ll only be sending more information and registration details to those women who have explicitly asked to hear more.

So, if you’d like the chance to join me live for this once-off training and get personalized advice (I'm leaving plenty of time for Q&A), please raise your hand by clicking on the link below.

Yes Girl, I’m in!

If you want to learn how to date with intention and ease, but can't make the time, it might be worth raising your hand anyway. Here’s why:

My team and I are crafting the email series for this event (which we’ll be sending out daily, Monday through Friday next week) as a coaching experience in itself (with special bonuses on the topic of dating for all women participating). 

There’ll also be a replay available for a limited time after the live event, but again, you'll only get that if you registered your interest through the link above.

If you think this isn't for you, don't worry, you don't have to do anything.

You will get one more email tomorrow (in which I’ll teach you the first and fundamental shift you need to make and where everything else starts) but you won't receive any more promotional emails for the Dating Masterclass on Monday, November 8.

In tomorrow’s email I’ll also include a step-by-step process that we use with women inside my group coaching program to actually make this shift so they can start transforming their dating reality.

It’s a pretty powerful tool that's capable of changing much more in your life than just your relationships with men, once you know how to put it into practice... so watch out for that in your inbox.

We’ll also touch upon this in the live upcoming Masterclass that the email series is going to end with, so if you’re interested in digging deeper and going with us on a journey, make sure to register your interest.

Alright, until then!

~ Anna "Great Men Are Out There" Rova



P.S. A disclaimer: definitely don’t raise your hand for this unless you're ready for a major paradigm shift, from soul-destroying and stressful dates to attracting a totally different caliber of men, as Anna S. experienced.

She went from feeling "emotionally burned out" and convinced her "man picker" was broken to experiencing things she never even believed to be possible on dates with truly masculine men (see below): 





Bridge Email 2 - Fri, Oct 29



(angle - fundamentals: dating tips & tricks won’t work until you get the fundamentals right 1. It’s all on YOU 2. Take radical responsibility. If you’re ready to do 1 & 2 and date in a different way etc. then raise your hand)



[subject line]



A: [Time-sensitive] Join my last live event of the year & fix your patterns with men.

B: [Live Dating Masterclass] Learn how to stop attracting all the “wrong” men



[body]

I used to live in a world where:

 "You can never trust men. They will always let you down. 

Men are afraid of commitment. They only want one thing.

It’s ‘nice guys’ vs. assholes and there’s nothing in between. 

Men cheat, lie, are lazy, selfish, and so on and so on..."

So, what changed?

The answer is: me.

My belief system about men, relationships and myself has transformed and, as a result, my reality with men has changed as well.Today, I want to share with you the step-by-step process of exactly how I did it, and how I coach women through the same journey inside my Claimed program every day.

But before we dive into that, a quick reminder that I’m running a Live Dating Masterclass on Monday, November 8th at 9 PM EST. If you missed my email yesterday, read it here. It’ll give you some important context for today’s email.If a whole new dating reality sounds like just what the (love) doctor prescribed, don’t forget to raise your hand by clicking this link for more information (even if the live event time doesn’t suit you, you’ll still benefit from the special series of coaching emails on the topic of dating that I’ll be sending out daily, Monday through Friday next week (including some special bonuses.)

I’ll also be sending a replay link for the live masterclass afterwards, but only to those women who’ve raised their hand. If this doesn’t sound like your cup of tea, no worries. This will be the last email where I mention it.

Alright, on to the juicy bit... 

Fundamentals & The Mirror Principle

When you look into the mirror every day, what do you see?

Do you, like me, look at yourself and see a few things you’re not really happy about?

Perhaps it’s that pimple on your forehead.

Perhaps it’s the few extra kilos around your waist.

Maybe it’s time to get those eyebrows done.

And maybe, it’s finally time to commit to a laser hair removal package.

Mirrors serve an important purpose — to show us what’s on the other side.

To show us what’s not working. 

Of course, there are some things we cannot change: the family, society and culture we were born into, the generational trauma we inherited, the destructive patterns we absorbed…

But we can change how we see them. 

We can observe, evaluate, and adjust based on what we see reflected back to us.

So why is it so hard to just look in the mirror when it comes to our lives and our relationships, especially with men?

Because it means facing two difficult truths that many of us would rather not accept. 

Together, they make up the first fundamental shift you need to make if you want to transform not only your dysfunctional dating patterns, but the whole reality you are living in, especially with men. 

This is where everything starts: 



EVERYTHING in your life is a reflection of who you are deep down inside.



Read that again and sit with it for a moment… 



Do you agree? 

Your belief systems. Your values. Your feelings, thoughts and certain behaviors.

And when you take charge and actively work on adjusting, reframing and upgrading your beliefs — everything changes.

The same thing applies to your relationships with men.

The trick is that a lot of these suckers are subconscious.

Like deep underwater currents, they influence the direction of our lives and shape the quality of our relationships.

And, as we go through life, our lived experience shapes and solidifies them even further until they become so ingrained in us that we keep attracting and reliving the same patterns over and over again.

The solution?



Responsibility means taking things over, being in charge and taking ownership of something. 

Radical responsibility? It's freaking radical! It's not, "I'm just taking responsibility." But it's willing to look at the hard stuff, the sometimes ugly truth and being ready to do something about it. It's putting everything on the table. (It’s also essential for great leadership but that’s another subject.)

Taking radical responsibility also means that deep down inside you know that you have everything you need (or have all the resources to figure out a way) to make whatever you want happen. 

When I look into the mirror of my life and I don’t like what I see…

I take ownership. I ask questions, I go within and I start adjusting.

And I adjust and adjust until I start liking what I see in that reflection.

I don’t wait for someone else to come in and fix my problems, be it the government, my father or society at large. 

I make shit happen. I live in the present moment. I create my own reality of men, relationships and my life. So, if you’re looking at your dating life and see only feminine men or assholes around — it’s time to go within and ask yourself:

“Mirror, mirror on the wall, why am I attracting these types of men? What am I thinking, doing and feeling that is drawing these feminine or asshole men to me? And, most importantly, what am I believing?”

And here is where the actual work begins.

The good news is that you’re more than halfway there.

You’re more than halfway to liking what you see in the mirror.

Because shedding light on what’s not working and then going back within to explore, ask questions, excavate and reframe is what women who are able to attract the men they want and truly deserve do.

But that’s not what most women do when it comes to fixing their unwanted patterns with men.

Most women look into the mirror of their dating life, don’t like what they see and what do they do?

They blame the patriarchy, they blame all men for being assholes or not wanting to carry the load, they blame feminine women, they blame their parents, past boyfriends…

They start looking for ways to appear prettier, sexier, fuller, blonder, tighter, more round up…

In a quest to become that perfect shape, size, color, texture, and flavor that the men will like.

They find external reasons as to why their dating life isn’t working. And then they top it off with external facades to masquerade what’s really going inside.

But see, we don’t even need a physical mirror or to go climb some volcanic mountains to look into the reflection of our life and our romantic relationships to see what’s not working.

The good news is that it’s all there.

Right there in front of our eyes.

Everything that’s not going well in your life — it’s all on the surface.

All the less than desirable men you’re attracting — they’re right there.

Take a look at your latest received messages and you’ll see what I mean.

Check in with your feelings about the guys you’ve been on dates with in the last month — it’s all there.

And take a look at how many men are chasing you instead of you chasing them — it’s all there.

And that’s great news because now that you know what’s not working you can start digging.

Thank you, mirror. 🙏🏻

When I looked into my own mirror it was quite painful.

He wasn’t calling as often as I wanted him to.

He wasn’t planning our weekend getaways.

He wasn’t showing signs of wanting to be together forever.

He wasn’t overly concerned if I wasn’t available.

He just wasn’t there…

Like I needed him to be there.

Casual sex?

Sure.

Wanna go out for drinks?

Sure.

Wanna go for a quick trip to the nearby island?

No problemo! That should be fun.

He wasn’t committed.

He wasn’t ready, willing and available for a relationship.

And, of course, I didn’t know back then what I know now.

It took me a few painful years to figure out how the mirror works.

But you don’t need to wait that long. I have reverse engineered what I did to eventually (like three years later) meet my now (masculine) husband who I created a family with:



And you have everything you need to start the feedback loop and change the pattern of men in your life. 

Here are your steps:

5 Steps to Fixing Your Dating Patterns with Men Using The Mirror Principle



Take a piece of paper and a pen.This whole process should take 10-15 mins.





Observe: Write down 5-10 names of the men you’re currently seeing/dating/interacting with in close proximity. Also look at the men in your life that you’re not engaged with romantically: male family members, male colleagues, or simply men on the street.

Identify: Identify the things in them that you’re not really comfortable with and would like to change. Examples are “He is not calling me back - he’s not emotionally available” or “He is afraid of commitment. He treats me like I’m the last one on his priority list.” In my case, he wasn’t committed, not there, and didn't take things seriously. I was just an accessory. (Look out for patterns and similarities between the guys who might be ghosting you and the father who is emotionally unavailable.)

Reflect: Ask yourself how might you be mirroring this in your own belief system or life? How might you be attracting that? If he’s not emotionally available - ask yourself “How might I not be emotionally available? Deep down inside why might I not be completely open and available? What are my fears here?” 



If he’s afraid of commitment ask yourself “How and why might I not be ready for a relationship yet? Why might be I afraid of commitment? What’s stopping me from being fully committed?” Write down the beliefs you’ve identified. I realized that I just wanted to be loved. I had low self-esteem. I didn’t know how to relax into my femininity and let men take care of me.

Adjust: Sit with what you have uncovered and consider what you might be able to do right now in order to change what you see reflected. For example, I decided to raise my standards, set some serious boundaries, and started finding ways to show myself more love, to get out of my head and into my heart, to be more present, to welcome and embrace all of myself, knowing that even the stuff I didn’t was there for a reason (All this stuff we do in our Claimed Group Coaching Program.)

Begin the feedback loop: Repeat this process over and over until you get the result you desire. The Mirror Principle is always at play, just like polarity (which I’ll dig into further in my coaching emails to those women who have raised their hands for my Dating Masterclass). The journey is never over. It’s a feedback loop that never ends. Even when you’re married. I still work on this daily. But, trust me, it’s worth it. Because it works!



Reply to this email and let me know the current reality with men, the beliefs you’ve identified and your reflections on them. If there’s something I think my team and I can do to help, we’ll send more resources your way. 



Your replies will also inform our email mini-series next week as well as the content of the upcoming live event. (Note: we will always respect your privacy and never divulge any sensitive personal information.) 



If you want to be part of the mini-series and attend the live event, click on this link here so we know to send you all the details.



Alright, keep doing the work, girlfriends. I’ll be right here, cheering you on.

~ Anna “Mirror, Mirror” Rova



P.S. No matter how stuck you feel, no matter how much “stuff” you are carrying, it is totally possible to transform your reality. And I’m not just talking about your relationships with men. We see this every day with the women we work with. 



Realizing it’s all on you and taking radical responsibility is an ongoing, life-expanding journey that’s capable of setting some major shifts in motion when it comes to your relationships with family members, money, career, colleagues... and more, exactly as Vee, another one of my amazing clients, is discovering:



Registration Sequence (only to interested segment)

Registration Email 1: Mon, Nov 1

[subject line]



[DATING MASTERCLASS: 1/5] Become a magnet for great men



[body]



{You are receiving this because you raised your hand for my Dating Masterclass. If you've changed your mind, that's okay. You can one-click unsubscribe from this series at any time. You'll also find a full unsubscribe link in the footer of this email.}

Welcome to this special 5-Part Dating Mini-Course & Live Masterclass, woo-hoo!

In Part 1 (this email) we’ll outline the symptoms (the kinds of things you may be experiencing in your current dating reality) and look at some symptom fixes (that you can get started on today).

I'm so glad that you've joined us, so honored to have your attention and so excited to kick off this journey together!

But, OMG, before I forget, here are all the important details you need to know:

The Live Masterclass, Become a Magnet for Great Men - Transform Your Dating Reality & Attract the Men You Really Want will take place on Monday, November 8 at 9 PM EST

Click here (or on the image below) to register









I seriously cannot wait to jump on live with you and share all the goodies I’ve got in store (plus, do some live coaching and answer all the questions you’ve got on the subject).

Remember, even if you can’t make it next Monday, you still get this mini-course plus the replay and a bonus gift at the end, so make sure to confirm your registration.

Preparing for this event and creating these coaching emails is also re-inspiring me on all things dating and men (which is exactly how I want you to feel about dating by the time we’re done).

Damn, it feels good to engage my brain again on something other than a new baby! So, let’s get started…

The mirror exercise I shared in the previous email is designed to work as a symptom trigger, asking you to pay attention, to take a closer look at your patterns with men. (I highly recommend you go back and do that 5-step exercise before you continue. And, by the way, I’m curious to know. What came up for you?)

Let’s look first at the typical symptoms women experience in dating:



Dating feels like a chore. You’re frustrated, burned out and stuck in the land of “wrong” men.

You are attracting assholes (hello, toxic masculinity!) and emotionally unavailable men who don’t want to commit. Who are not ready for a relationship. Who are just interested in…you know what.

Or you are attracting “nice guys” who are very much in their feminine energy and would rather let you make all the decisions and lead the relationship forward.

Not sexy at all.

It’s important to understand that what you see on the surface (the tip of the iceberg) is just symptoms.

And these symptoms serve a purpose. It’s like with any type of disease or imbalance that you have in the body...

How do you know when you need to go to the doctor?

Because you experience symptoms.

You might have frequent migraines, or your stomach aches, or your ankle hurts…

Whatever it is, your body is communicating. It’s showing you that something is not working.

In the same way, your dating reality and what you attract in life in terms of men and relationships is showing you what’s not working.

Based on my own experience, years coaching women professionally in personal growth, as well as in the dating and relationships niche, I found that most of the stuff that’s out there providing relationship advice and coaching for women is missing two fundamental pieces of the puzzle:

Symptom Fixes

Root Cause Fixes

And these two go together.

Sometimes, it’s enough to fix just the symptoms.

It’s what the majority of dating coaches will do, with things like “ultimate” texting guides or “how to get the guy” manuals and so on...

This is all some women need, and that’s why all of this dating advice does work and will help if you have a healthy belief system, a strong sense of self-worth, and you intuitively and instinctively know how to “be in you feminine” (as some women do).

To come back to the disease analogy, symptom fixes are where we have to start. That’s exactly where a doctor will start as well. Because it’s a process of elimination, right?

If it's a simple symptom fix, why go for all the tests and scans and treatments and so on, right?

So, that’s what I want to address today (and I will expand on this much more in the Masterclass).

Symptom fixes

Here’s a real-life example to help you understand what I’m talking about here.

I have struggled for a long time with adult acne.

The symptom for me? Acne on my skin. The symptom fixes for me? Just go for facials, or just use this skincare product, or just use these antibiotics or this hormonal pill as prescribed by this dermatologist.

But, as I have realized, every body is different (just like every woman’s belief systems are different, based on different elements).

So, these symptom fixes might work for some women. But they did not work for me.

When I started digging deeper and deeper, I realized that, actually, I was dealing with a root cause.

The root cause for me? My diet, what I put in my body (which I never thought was related to acne, because you think, well, this is a skin problem, so let’s fix the skin).

For me, this is actually a gut problem.

So, for a lot of women, dating is the same. It’s not about knowing what to say or how to behave or what to wear, but it’s all about learning how to dancing the dance of polarity and looking deep down inside enough to see why you’re attracting what you’re attracting.

And the further you dig, the more you uncover...

On my journey with adult acne, it’s been the same.

It’s not just about my gut and diet, but also my personal growth. I’m actually going for a healing session myself this week with a specialized coach to help me understand what is the subconscious belief system that I’m carrying that’s manifesting in this way in my body.

Sometimes to fix the disease and not just the symptoms, we need to keep looking deeper and deeper still... undercovering layer upon layer of root causes (which is part of what we’ll work on in the Live Masterclass).

But it’s important to start with the symptom fixes because, even if your problem goes deeper, these will open your eyes, shift your perspective and nudge you toward the direction you want.

And when it comes to dating, the symptom fixes are all about knowing the rules of the game:



To experience another reality in dating, these are the first 3 steps you need to take.

The deep work of root causes comes in later, and, to really do it properly requires working with someone. (Well, I did it by myself, but it took me 3 years.)

Here are the most important takeaways I want to give you today:

Your dating frustrations are just symptoms

Start with symptom fixes and go from there

To fix the symptoms of dating burnout/attracting the "wrong" men, you need to know the rules of the game. You need to: 1. Learn feminine/masculine polarity 2. Learn the 3 stages of the dating funnel. 3. Practice, practice, practice…

In Part 2 (tomorrow’s email), we’ll look at symptom fix #1 (feminine/masculine polarity) in much more detail.

I'll also share some action steps to help you start implementing it in your life.

Speaking of action steps... your action step for today? Reply to this email and let me know:

What are your symptoms? (What's not working for you?)

What have you already tried to fix it? (And how well has it worked?)

What do you think might be the root cause?

Taking the time to reflect on and answer these questions is an important part of this process.

It will also help me to understand more about the specific challenges you are facing so I can make sure I’m focusing on the right details in my upcoming emails, as well as the Masterclass itself.

Quick reminder: if you haven’t already, click here to save your seat for next week’s live event. (Remember, even if the time doesn’t work for you, registering will ensure you get the replay and the special gift I’ll be sending out afterward.)

I’m looking forward to hearing from you on those action steps!

Alright, until tomorrow. 

~ Anna



P.S. Symptoms suck.

They’re uncomfortable, exhausting, and persistent… because they serve a purpose.

They are there to remind you again and again that something’s out of balance, until you finally pay attention.

Until then, you'll also be missing out. Missing out on how much FUN dating can be! And on all the perks that come with knowing the rules of the game.

As a sneak peek of things to come, here's what dating can look like when you embrace the work (starting with this mini-course) and commit to showing up for yourself:

“Things are going amazing for me. I have a few people in my funnel and completely see a difference in the men I’m dating. Most importantly, the way I handle situations simply by just leaning back and using my feminine energy. This is so much fun! I can’t imagine ever feeling anything different other than this feminine energy going forward in life.”

This is the dating reality that’s possible for you, too:











Registration Email 2: Tues, Nov 2

(angle - polarity, the key to becoming a man magnet)



[subject line]



[DATING MASTERCLASS: 2/5] You gotta learn to salsa



[body]



{You are receiving this because you raised your hand for my Dating Masterclass. If you've changed your mind, that's okay. You can one-click unsubscribe from this series at any time. You'll also find a full unsubscribe link in the footer of this email.}

Welcome to Part 2 of this 5-Part Mini-Course on How to Become a Magnet for Great Men!

Make sure you’ve read Part 1 before diving in below because what I’m about to share builds on that as a foundation. If you missed that, click here.

Quick reminder before we get to the juicy stuff: if you haven’t yet done so, here’s your link to register for the Live Masterclass on November 8th at 9 PM EST.

You’ll still get all five coaching emails leading up to the event even after you register, so no need to wait it out until the last minute. Book it into your calendar today.

Alright, housekeeping over.

Let’s dig in with Symptom Fix #1:

Masculine-Feminine Polarity

It’s a subject I’m obsessed with.

I've accumulated hours of research, writing, coaching, and podcast interviews with experts to gain a deep understanding of the sexual and energetic magnetism between the masculine and the feminine.

(It actually goes hand in hand with evolutionary psychology, another obsession of mine, which I might have mentioned, but we’ll get into that another time.)

I want you to understand the polarity principle first. Because once you do, it will change your perspective on just about everything. And that’s why it’s symptom fix #1.

From your relationships to how you feel in your own body.

You'll realize that this deep desire to surrender and just "be a girl" is natural. You're not crazy or a traitor to feminism for wanting to be taken care of, surrender and feel safe with a man.

It's like the magical gateway to another world. Once you get it and start seeing how it plays out, you'll never be able to go back.

And you won't want to.

Because you’ll be living in the land of great men who treat you like a queen.

To understand how polarity works, think of it like salsa dancing.



The masculine leads. The feminine follows.

He brings structure and direction. She brings beauty and depth.

And the dance is actually all about her!

But it won't work if both partners are trying to lead. It’ll be more like a disaster than a dance.

Because the masculine does not want to dance with the masculine.

The masculine only wants to dance with (lead) and is attracted to the feminine.

Read the above line again. It might give you clues as to why you’re not attracting masculine men. (Because you yourself are playing in the masculine bringing your amazing ambition, direction and the “balls” to your dating life. W.R.O.N.G. approach)

So, when it comes to dating and relationships, as a woman who wants to start attracting committed masculine men, you gotta learn to salsa, girl!

You need to learn to let go of control and be in your feminine when it comes to men and your dating game.

(I’ll teach you a bit more about how to do that in this email series and the Live Masterclass next Monday.)

But this goes against everything we've been taught as strong, independent women, right?

Never rely on a man. Never even trust one. You're in control of your own destiny.

Of course you are!

I'm not saying you should hand over the driver's wheel of your own life to your man.

This is what so many women don’t understand and why they feel repulsed by the message and the idea of polarity. They think it’s “going back to the 50s. Submitting to your man. Letting go of decision making power and being a damsel in distress.

This is not the modern, conscious polarity I advocate for and live my life by.

But I know you understand that if you’re reading this email. So keep reading...

In the dance of dating and of relationship, you have to let him lead. You have to learn how to switch from the getting-shit-done-where-is-this-going type of a woman into the relaxed, easy-going, playful woman.

From hard and angular into soft and playful.

From making all the plans and needing to know the answers to going with the flow, being okay with the unknown and willing to be surprised.

There's more to it, but these are the basics, which I'll build on in the Masterclass.

(I’ll also do a coaching session where you will actually feel how it is to actually experience this feminine pole in your body. Because the transformation that happens with women we work with is through the modality of embodiment, which I’ll touch upon then too.)

For now, just know that you don’t need to work so hard just to keep swimming in the shallow end of the man pond.

You are not broken.

You don’t have to suffer, question, and continue to ask yourself “what’s wrong with me?”

You can stop attracting all the “wrong” men and start making room for the right ones.

And, yes, they are out there. You're just not attracting them... yet.

But you’re about to learn how. You’re about to join the women out there who just seem to be magnets for great men.

Women who are just out there, sipping margaritas in a nice restaurant with a fabulous man. Or maybe even doing a bit of actual salsa... ;)



And that's why I'm here!

Tomorrow, I’ll send you Part 3 of this Mini-Course where you’ll understand why you’ve been a Nice Guy Magnet which feels “nice” in the beginning but very unattractive.

Oh, and register, register, register.

(You can check your time zone here.)

Okay, see you tomorrow!

~ Anna





P.S: I thought I’d share a few more real stories from some of the women enrolled in my Claimed program about how their dating life transformed after they got to know a few dating concepts and started applying them.

There is a different way to date that is fun and exciting and the proof is in the pudding. And there are plenty of amazing men out there.

Such as:

#1. Bike-carrying surfer guy:



#2. Drive 'cross the country to be with you guy:



And last, but not least…#3. Bald foodie with a heart of gold guy:















Registration Email 3 - Wed, Nov 3



(the problem with nice guys, my story, this was me: breakup, nice guys vs assholes - let’s start with the nice guys. Tell the story of the ex - use article and IGTV)

What is the goal/purpose of this email? What do we want women to understand/ get out of this email? For women to understand why they’re attracting assholes & know what they can do about it

What is the "how/action step" here? Healing the father wound & reframing limiting beliefs What is the next thing to read/do? CTA/open loop? Symptom Fix #2: the 3 stages of the dating funnel, learning to level the playing field & date like we’re shopping for shoes 





[subject line] 

[DATING MASTERCLASS: 3/5] No more Mr. Nice Guy



[body]



{You are receiving this because you raised your hand for my Dating Masterclass. If you've changed your mind, that's okay. You can one-click unsubscribe from this series at any time. You'll also find a full unsubscribe link in the footer of this email.}

Welcome to Part 3 of this 5-Part Mini-Course on How to Become a Magnet for Great Men!

===============

Before you continue, make sure you’ve read Part 1 and Part 2 because each part of this series builds on the previous ones as a foundation. 

And then I’m going to bring everything we cover here (and more) to life on Monday’s Masterclass so you can really see how it all fits together. This Mini-Course is like the outline for the colors we’ll be adding live, together. 

So, here’s your link to register for that in case you haven’t done so already.

===============

Okay, where were we?



That’s right! Polarity…



By the way, I just realized we forgot to include your action steps for Part 2, whoops! Like I said, we’re crafting this series from scratch in real time, so I guess these things are bound to happen.



These are the two questions I want you to ask yourself right now. Take a pen and paper and write down your answers:1. Where are you on the polarity spectrum? 

2. What kind of men are you attracting as a result?  



I’d love to hear what you uncovered. Reply and let me know!

Today, we’re sticking with polarity but diving a bit deeper now into the land of....nice guys vs. assholes. 



(Remember, we’re still on Symptom Fix #1 here. It’s so important to understand this before you start moving on to Symptom Fix #2 which is learning the 3 stages of the dating funnel because if you don’t, your dating funnel is just going to be full of...you guessed it...nice guys & assholes!)



Let’s start with the nice guys.



Once upon a time, I was dating a nice guy. 



In fact, he was the nicest guy every woman wants to be with (or so I thought). He was completely in love with me, would do anything for me, followed me all the way across the world when I got offered a job.



In short, my friends loved him, my family loved him and he was always nominated for the “best future dad” category. And, btw, I was the one who grabbed him by his shirt, dragged him out of a night club and kissed him. 



Basically, I started the relationship. He just fell into it because he didn’t have enough balls (masculine energy) to approach me or any other girl he really liked.



And, as it usually happens in a dynamic with the nice guy, I was also the one earning more, paying the bills and was in charge of major decisions. He was just following and agreeing with me most of the time because “I knew better.” 



I could walk all over him when things got rough. And deep down inside, I wasn’t proud to be seen with him (you know that feeling when you’re thrilled to introduce your man to everyone because you feel safe and secure with your man? I didn’t feel that with him.) 



And, as time went by, I stopped being attracted to him. Sex became dull and it felt like I was peentrating him and not the other way around.



I stopped respecting him. In fact, I don’t think I ever respected him. 



Also, as it happens, he was quite creative and artistic and I thought it was my job to guide him. 



So all that responsibility and the role I was playing in the relationship was quite… you guessed it… EXHAUSTING. I didn’t realize it at first but as years went by it kept piling up in my body and my energy. 



And, I, secretly, was always attracted to the alpha guy (as most of us are) - I just thought great masculine alpha guys do not exist. 



So I chose the lesser of the two evils (because a nice guy is also an “evil” in the sense that you won’t be happy with him.) 



I chose the nice guy instead of the asshole. 





Luckily, just before we were supposed to get engaged (a decision also driven by me,) I met a masculine man who swept me off my feet. That meeting was fatal for me because not only it broke the cycle of nice guys for me but also because my whole body of work around men and relationships started with that meeting!

What happened after is the pendulum has swung to the other side and I started attracting the asshole guys instead and thought “Well, if the nice guy isn’t for me, then let me try the other guys.” That hasn’t worked for me either and I’ll tell you about all that in the next email.  

So, if you find yourself in this same exact situation of attracting nice guys or this happens to be your pattern... 

Ask yourself these 3 questions to clarify where you stand:

1. How does it feel?

During one of our group coaching calls, a beautiful woman who’s joined our Claimed Program mentioned that she’s seeing this guy who’s extremely spiritual and talks about his feelings a lot (feminine energy man) and she wasn't sure whether she should continue seeing him.

When I asked her the question, “How does it feel to be around this guy?” she said something like, “Well, I think it’s great that he’s in touch with his feelings, but something isn’t right.

That “something isn’t right” is your clue into the fact that this guy might not be for you.

When you’re around a nice guy, it’s usually because it makes you feel like you’re in charge, like you’re loved. BUT the reality is that as much as you love being around a nice guy and it feels very cozy and comfortable like holding a cup of hot chocolate… you find yourself fantasizing about a glass of martini who is witty and charming. Who makes you laugh and with whom you feel a subtle tingle down there... 

Which leads me to the next question.

2. Are you attracted to this man?

If your answer is “kind of” or “sometimes” then your answer is NO. I’m talking about the “grab-my-ass-and-throw-me-against-the-wall” kind of attraction and not the “too-shy-or-too-scared-to-make-a-move” kind of an attraction. There is nothing more frustrating than you wanting him to just go for it and him hesitating. Yuck! 

3. Who’s holding more space for who?

Do you feel like you can completely trust this man? 

Do you feel safe and protected with this man? Who’s going to deal with the bad guys if shit hits the fan? If the answer is most likely YOU then you also know that you’re with a nice guy.

So, by answering these 3 questions you know that:

1. This relationship dynamic with Mr. Nice Guy doesn’t feel good to you

2. You’re not attracted to this man and 

3. You can’t completely trust him, let go of control, and just let him lead…

In this scenario you are in the driver’s seat.

The polarity is reversed. You are in your masculine and he’s in his feminine.

And so if you do not stop or reverse this dynamic from the beginning, you might end up being in a long-term relationship, maybe even married to this kind of a man.

In our Claimed program we have lots of women who’ve been married to these men. And walked all over them. Told them what to do. Had children with them. And then left them. 

And let me tell ya, after 20 years of bossing and parenting not only your kids around but also your man, it gets so exhausting that sometimes it becomes a physical malady. 

As women, if we understand this and are clear on the polarity dynamic that we want, we can set our boundaries and communicate them to a man.

We can lean back and allow him the space to step forward and step up.

So how do we do that? How do we stop being in the driver seat and, instead, allow him to take the reins?

Here are some practical tips I teach women on how you can connect to your femininity on a daily basis:



5 things you can do today to embrace your feminine energy:



Give yourself permission to relax, to just be without doing: Schedule gaps into your calendar. Spend time in nature, particularly close to water (feminine element). Practice meditation. 

Dance: Move your body often & freely in a non-linear way, getting out of your head and dropping into your heart and womb space. Let your body lead.

Make creativity a habit in your life: creativity and play is a feminine energy, so banish your inner critic & get creative at least once a week. (The Artist’s Way is an amazing resource.)

Track your cycle: study, observe & work with your menstrual cycle & the cycles of the moon. Honor your mood & your energy. (Listen to Episode 108 of the Claimed Podcast.)

Turn down the volume on the outside: (so you can turn up the volume on the inside) information overwhelm drowns out our embodied feminine intuition. Give yourself a break from scrolling the news & social media. Spend time in silence. 

I can’t wait to tie all these pieces together, live with you on Monday’s Masterclass. Have you registered yet? Click here if not.



And, if you’re a woman who’s been attracting masculine guys who are in the driver seat but they’re just not emotionally available (assholes as we call them,) stay tuned for tomorrow’s email where we’ll explore that dynamic and give you some tips on how to start breaking this pattern.



What’s resonated with you the most from this series so far? Just hit reply to share your biggest A-ha moments and ask those lingering questions.



(My team and I will answer every email.)



Back tomorrow!



~ Anna



P.S. There's nothing like a taste of things to come when we need a little motivation to do the work. So, here’s what some women have experienced after they started implementing this knowledge about polarity and reconnecting to their feminine energy:



﻿[INSERT SCREENSHOT TESTIMONIALS]



Registration Email 4 - Thurs, Nov 4



(the real reason you're surrounded by emotionally unavailable assholes - men as mirrors revisited + reframing limiting beliefs exercise)



[subject line]



[DATING MASTERCLASS: 4/5] Every man is a teacher (even the assholes)



[body]



{You are receiving this because you raised your hand for my Dating Masterclass. If you've changed your mind, that's okay. You can one-click unsubscribe from this series at any time. You'll also find a full unsubscribe link in the footer of this email.}

Welcome to Part 4 of this 5-Part Mini-Course on How to Become a Magnet for Great Men!

===============

Here is Part 1,  Part 2 & Part 3 of this series if you want to catchup. They provide important context and grounding for what we’ll cover today.

Then, on Monday’s Masterclass we’ll solidify everything you’re learning in the email series. Have you registered? Here’s the link again. ===============

In the last email we spoke about nice guys. Today, we’re moving on to the assholes.



I’m talking about the guys who are emotionally unavailable, guys who ghost or engage in other kinds of undesirable behaviours.



Assholes are men who are in their masculine but not in a healthy way. And, if you are attracting these types of men, the good news is: you are in your feminine. You’re on the right track in terms of polarity. However, if you are attracting the unhealthy masculine in men, we need to look at your unhealthy feminine. 



It all comes back to The Mirror Principle I spoke about in a recent email. (Click here to read that - there’s a 5-step exercise at the end that I highly recommend you complete before continuing here.)In short, The Mirror Principle, means that whatever you’re not satisfied with in terms of the men you’re attracting in your dating life is a direct reflection of your belief system about men, relationships and yourself. 



Let’s dive a little deeper…



Beliefs are formed convictions and thoughts that we have ingrained and rooted so deeply that they drive our behaviour and outlook. They are what we hold true and they can either be expanding or limiting. 



Some beliefs are obvious and conscious, but many, especially our most deeply held limiting beliefs, are subconscious. They have been created and reinforced by our lived experiences and, often, by the shame we carry in connection to those experiences.



These suckers are usually the root causes of our dating & relationship issues with men but I’ll talk about this on the webinar more in depth.







Limiting beliefs are basically what’s subconsciously driving your patterns of attracting emotionally unavailable men, men who’re toxic, and, sometimes, even those nice guys we spoke about in the previous email.



You see, we always attract our equals in terms of our energy, secure or fragile sense of self. Every situation teaches us something. 



Every man we meet is a teacher.



Beliefs like “I’m not good enough,” “I don’t deserve love” and “I’m not worthy” are really what’s at the bottom of all unhealthy interactions with men, with other people and with everything else. 



So if you are attracting men who are emotionally unavailable, chances are you are emotionally unavailable yourself but it’s very hard to see this on the surface.



On discovery calls women tell us all the time “But I am ready for a relationship! I’ve done SO MUCH work on myself! I’m the whole package!” 



But you see, what these women are not aware of is that deep down inside, below all the layers of achievements and accomplishments that make them feel like “the whole package” there is still a belief system that might have been passed down from her mother, who all her life struggled to trust men - that men can’t be trusted. So she is, indeed, the whole package - but a package that doesn’t trust men and has abandonment issues.

And this is exactly the serious deep work we do in our Claimed Program and why our clients see amazing results. Once the light is shown in the shadows, these beliefs can’t stay in the shadow anymore. They come to the surface and dissolve.



I will share a few things with you today as well as the live masterclass on Monday.



In order for you to shift and transform in powerful ways so you can attract the men you really want and have the relationship you really want, here are the 2 things I recommend working on already today:



Healing The Father WoundOur early experiences hold the keys to our deepest and most limiting beliefs and our fathers are our first and most important encounters with men.



I grew up seeing unhealthy dynamics between men and women in marriages. I grew up seeing men who don’t embody their masculinity in a healthy way, who are emotionally unavailable, who cheat, and who are assholes.



After years of doing personal growth and diving deep into relationships, I knew that healing my relationship with dad was the key to fixing my relationship with men. If you’re curious to hear more of that story, listen to Episode 178 of the Claimed Podcast.Of course, we learn a whole lot from our mothers as well, but that’s a whole other dynamic that deserves looking at it in detail (something we do inside our group coaching program).From dads we learn and form beliefs about who men are, how they behave and how relationships look like. We learn what men want, how they treat women and see masculinity for what it is in him.



And we interpret how we should be treated as women through him as well.We also see what love is.

And what love is not.We learn how to trust men and whether they deserve to be trusted at all.And then we mimic and mirror that behavior in our relationships.Lots of things to unpack and discover in that “daddy, please love me” bag of beliefs we take on into our adult life as women.If we want to attract a mature masculine man, we have to get our “daddy issues” sorted before we meet him. We have to realize that men won’t fix, replace or erase those dynamics and they will never replace our fathers. These are two separate (important) roles that need to be engaged differently.

So, if your relationship with men is less than desirable and your relationship with dad is quite rocky, there is a connection there.

Ask yourself these questions:

>> How is your relationship with your father? Positive, negative or neutral?

>> What are the constrictions there?

>> What are you still holding within and can’t let go of?

>> What do you want to say but have never given yourself permission to?

>> And, most importantly, how is all this affecting your relationship with men?



2. Reframe your beliefs about men. 

Your beliefs about men are what is driving your pattern of attracting the men you’re seeing in your life. Men are our mirrors. So, if you want to change what you see “in the mirror” you have to start digging into your belief system about men. You have to look at the root cause of your dynamics with men and reframe that into much more powerful beliefs that will serve you.Here is my 4-step process to reframing your limiting beliefs about men and the questions you can use to go through it:

1. Identify limiting belief (+ payoff)

2. Reframe into new belief

3. Release old belief

4. Embody new belief

Take a pen and a piece of paper and go through the following steps: 

Step 1: Identifying limiting beliefs + Payoff

Limiting belief:

Actions are created by this thought/belief:

Identify the payoff:

How am I right/better/justified in thinking this thought?

Why am I unable to change?

What do I think/do/get that makes me feel better about myself when I think this thought & act it out in the world?

What is deep below this?

Step 2: Reframe into new belief

Who would you be without this belief?

How would you show up in the world without this belief?

What is a more positive thought or belief your body wants to believe?

What action would this new belief have you take?

Step 3 & 4: Release old belief and embody new belief using feminine embodiment practices and coaching. This is where I come into the picture as your coach and your guide and I’ll take you through an exercise in the Live Masterclass to help you get started here.



Take the time you need to work through all these questions. If you don’t have a lot of time to spare, just pick one section for now and come back to the others later.And remember, it all starts with you. Reply to this email and let me know what came up for you and how men have been your teachers. I can't wait to hear your realizations! Also, take note of any questions you have around what we’ve covered so far. You can either include those in your email or save them for the Live Q&A on Monday.



In Part 5 (the final part of this series), we’ll get into the fun stuff: Symptom Fix #2 - learning the 3 stages of the dating funnel. Knowing the rules of the game and how to play by them is gonna change the way you think about and experience dating. 



We’re going from job interviews to shopping for shoes, girl!  



See you then.



~ Anna



P.S: The work on limiting beliefs is crucial. It's what's really going to allow you to shift and transform in powerful ways

And because I know how helpful and encouraging it can be to see how it's actually done in practice, I wanted to share how Stephanie worked through the exact same exercise and then shared it in our private Claimed group.

I hope this framework will be able to help move through and overcoming your beliefs about men so you can start becoming a magnet for great men.

Because you’re worthy.

And because you deserve it.

So, let's take a look:









P.P.S. When I asked Stephanie whether I could share her realizations, here is what she wrote:





Registration Email 5 - Fri, Nov 5

(Symptom Fix #2: the 3 stages of the dating funnel & how to level the dating playing field)



What is the goal/purpose of this email? To outline the 3 stages of the dating funnel & root causesWhat do we want women to understand/ get out of this email? Consolidating all they’ve learned so far and a good grounding on stage 1 of the dating funnel What is the next thing to read/do? CTA/open loop? CTA: questions related to stage 1: If you were to imagine their ideal man, where would he hang out, how would you meet one another? 2. What are you currently doing to maximize your chances of meeting great men? Or are you leaving things to chance? ) and open loop: stage 2 & 3 + root causes in more detail...



[subject line]



[DATING MASTERCLASS PART 5/5] What’s in the dating magic box?

[body]

{You are receiving this because you raised your hand for my Dating Masterclass. If you've changed your mind, that's okay. You can one-click unsubscribe from this series at any time. You'll also find a full unsubscribe link in the footer of this email.}

Welcome to the final part of this Mini-Course on How to Become a Magnet for Great Men. 

===============

If you’re joining late or need to catch up, here’s a summary of what we’ve covered so far: 

Part 1: Your Dating Frustrations - Symptoms & Symptom Fixes 

Part 2: Masculine/Feminine Polarity (Symptom Fix #1) 

Part 3: The Problem with Nice Guys & Why You’re Attracting Them (Reversed Polarity) 

Part 4: Surrounded by Assholes? How to Turn Them Into Teachers (Revisiting The Mirror Principle & Reframing Limiting Beliefs) And, now that we’ve got our grounding in place, we can finally open our dating magic box and take a peek, so to speak… 

Part 5: The 3 Stages of the Dating Funnel (Symptom Fix #2) - read below ⬇ On Monday’s Live Masterclass we’ll tie all this together, bring it to life, plus add a few more dimensions we haven’t yet explored. (Remember those Root Causes & Root Cause Fixes, for example?)

You’ll only get the link to join us live if you’ve registered, so do that here. 

===============

🚨 Breaking News: dating is not supposed to feel like a chore, a job interview, or a source of anxiety and burnout.

Dating is supposed to be fun!



Because, let's be honest, dating is a game and games are a lot more fun when you know the rules and how to play by them.

Even the women who say “I’m done with playing games” actually mean they’re done with playing the wrong game but they’ll be totally up for the right game with the right man.

This is exactly how men date.

And they have an advantage over us because they've been learning how to play the game, googling how to “get girls” from the moment they started having wet dreams (which is many, many years ago unless you’re into teenagers 😉).

You see, it all comes back to evolutionary psychology.

I spent a lot of time researching this whole “polarity” thing trying to answer the question of “why?!”

Why does polarity exist in couples?

What is it driven by?

I wanted to go deep and understand it. I couldn’t just take it as a spiritual concept David Deida says is true. I have an inquisitive mind like that. When I don’t get something I dig deep until I find the answer.

I needed to know why.

Why are men and women so different in their mating dance, in the way they show up in dating, in relationships, in the way they behave, feel and even think?

Just saying “men are from Mars and women are from Venus” wasn’t good enough for me.

During all my research, coaching and my own lived experience, I realized that most of these differences are really nature, rather than nurture. And, as much as cultural and societal beliefs and factors play their role here, nature really takes over.

The short answer to the why polarity exists, why women yearn to feel safe and secure in a relationship and are attracted to alpha men with the most amount of resources is…

Parental investment and its impacts on evolutionary psychology (this is what’s in the magic box that you’re now getting the keys to and so many women don’t!)

As long as women are the ones to conceive and carry children inside their bodies and then nurture them - polarity will always exist.

It’s driven by our evolutionary psychology which is basically a “program” inside our brains that has been developing for thousands of years and evolving to survive.

And our psychology today hasn’t had the time to adapt to the modern paradigms of “women in power” or “women leaning in.” Because for thousands of years men were the ones to go out there to hunt and bring food on the table while women took care of infants and cooked the food that men killed.

There is no leaning in to kill a tiger when you’ve got an infant in your carrier.

Our psychology hasn’t had enough time to adapt to men sitting at home and sharing 50% of the housework. Stay at home dads is a phenomenon that has emerged only in the last few decades, which is nothing in evolutionary terms.

Our bodies and brains are still running on the program of hunter-gatherer societies where HE is the protector and the provider and SHE is the creator of life and the nurturer.

The sooner we stop fighting this programming, the happier all of us will become.

“But, Anna, we live in a different time now. It’s time to evolve past these archaic, sexist and patriarchal views. We can and should change this programming! Now!”

I agree that we can change this programming (it will take some hundreds of thousands of years though) and we’ve started to.

But it doesn’t really work.

Why?

Because #biology.

And this is the paradox we live in today. Women today have the biggest opportunities and they’re having the most amount of independence they’ve ever had. But they’re more unhappy than ever.

Remember the woman who said “I don’t understand! I’m the whole package! Where is he?”

Why?

Because now women have to step up and play the masculine role AND the feminine one too (something never ever seen before) - conceive, carry, birth and nurture children at the same time. And they feel bitter and angry that it all falls on them. That men “are not playing their part.”

But men can’t play “their part” because it is not theirs to play.

Men don’t ovulate and menstruate every month. Men do not cycle. Men do not give 2-3 years of their life to a child. Men do not have wombs that create and grow life or breasts that produce milk.

So again, as long as women are the ones to conceive and carry children inside their bodies and then nurture them, polarity will always exist.

You can try and set up rules to have 50% of women on executive boards and male dominated industries and do all kinds of other things all day long but you’re not going to change the fact that my body takes over when I get pregnant and have to birth and nurse my children.

I have to let go. I have to give up. Become vulnerable and “weak.” I have to trust my man and my village to take care of me while I take care of the baby. I have experienced it again during my second pregnancy and child.

All this hits you like a ton of bricks when you get to the point of not being able to get back to work and having to rely on your man’s income for a year or two. And, if so it happens that you’re married to a nice guy and have been paying all the bills and earning all the money, your relationship is going to go to hell because you’re not going to be able to do that anymore.

No wonder divorce rates are the highest during the first year after a baby arrives. It all makes sense now.

But hang on - even if you never have children or choose not to - this is all programmed inside. Something we can’t change.

"Okay, okay, Anna, I understand… Parental investment, evolutionary psychology, polarity… What does this have to do with dating being fun?!”

Glad you asked. Let me get back on track.

Now that we understand evolutionary psychology and why polarity exists, let’s talk about the mating dance.

Dating.

Introducing new concept #1: Sexual Selection.



When we are dating, we’re dancing and sexually selecting each other.

We’re looking for a life-long partner in this dance. And not just a life-long partner but an attractive one at that. The one we can also rely and depend on when shit hits the fan or when you’re 8 months pregnant.

Because humans are also animals, we dance the mating dance like animals.

Males always peacock and show off and compete and fight to get the attention of the most attractive female so that he can impregnate her (and a lot of other females as well) and pass on his genes.

The bigger one, the faster one, the stronger one and the richer one has the attention. Because the one with the most amount of resources can secure her future and the future of her children.

That is why females deep down inside are attracted to the one who wins, who is confident, who has ambition and a plan. Who knows where he’s going and has a mission and a purpose. His masculine energy is irresistible.

So she actually doesn’t have to do much besides looking like her most “fertile” self. And “fertile” changes every 50-100 years depending on cultural, environmental, political and other external factors.

We show up at a bar looking fabulous and ready to “dance” and men approach. They come at you peacocking and showing off. Their resources, their muscles, their money - whatever they can to lure you in, pursue and court.

It’s in their DNA. In their blood. In their psyche. They're addicted to it. As they should because, otherwise, they’ll die and never produce an offspring.

So they know how to play the game of dating and pursuing. If they can’t find it on google because somehow a link has been missed in their evolutionary brain, they’ll find it in themselves.

In a way, their life mission is to figure out what women want, get them into bed and put a baby in there. 😉

(If this is harsh language, I apologize. But if you’re reading this email I know you appreciate my straightforward approach. I hope you find it funny as well. 😜)

So, to level out the playing field and play the game of dating on equal terms (not financially or household equal terms), you need to know the rules of the game.

But what do most women do these days when it comes to finding a great man?

They leave things to chance.

They focus on their career and then they wake up one day to realize that the clock is ticking…

They have everything they wanted, and now the only thing that they can't find is a man.

We gotta fix this asap.

Introducing new concept #2: The 3 Stages of The Dating Funnel.

This, my friends, is also symptom fix #2.

The dating funnel means not leaving things to chance. It’s a tool that you can use to avoid being stuck in the situation of not knowing how the game works. It’s how you maximize your chances of meeting that committed, masculine man of your dreams.

But, remember, your dating funnel is only going to work if you first understand polarity and practice leaning into your feminine energy (see Part 3).

That’s going to get you the masculine men. But if you want committed masculine men, you need to work on exploring your patterns with men, the mirror principle, and reframing your limiting beliefs (see Part 4).

(I’ll dive more into all this, offer you some coaching and answer all your questions live on the masterclass.)

Because otherwise your funnel is going to be defective. You’re going to be a magnet for all the “wrong” types of men.

So, here are the 3 Stages of The Dating Funnel.

Stage 1: Awareness (exposure to potential suitors) Stage 2: Consideration (dating) Stage 3: Conversion (partner)



And after that? It’s practice, practice, practice…

Today, we’re going to focus on the first stage (then in the masterclass, we’ll move on to the 2nd and 3rd because these are the ones that tend to trip women up the most).



Think about exposure as declaring yourself, showing yourself to the world.

Because, honestly, if you just sit at home and wait for your man to come knocking on your door, it's not going to happen.

I mean, you will be attracting men. Absolutely (if you are in your feminine energy). But you've got to do something extra to make sure that you are being exposed to the best quality men that you want to have in your life.

You’re giving these men a chance to do what they do best, which is compete for your attention. And then you're choosing the ones you like as you move into stages 2 and 3.

Again, it’s evolutionary psychology in play: you create your dating funnel, men compete.

And, to be honest with you, it just brings a lot of fun into your life. Being surrounded by masculine energy is the best gift that you can give yourself. So give yourself that gift.

Okay, Anna, got it. Expose yourself to the kind of men I really want, but where, when, and how do I even do that?

It’s quite simple, really.



A very practical thing: a lot of women, especially in today's connected world, are always on our phones, right? Checking emails on the go, scrolling social media, replying to messages, doing tasks and booking spa appointments, and thus, we are closed off to potential suitors and exposure.

So put the phone down.

Look up. Look around.

Smile.

Lose the poker face, or the resting bitch face. (You know, the one that’s never going to get you a man who’ll want to take you to the end of the world and back. Or to the Maldives. Or, at least, to a nice fancy dinner and pay for it.)

Practice being open in your energy. Signal that you are open to being approached.

Another tip is, when you walk on the street or wherever you are, just slow down.

A woman who's on a mission walking fast, poker face on, headphones in her ears, looking down at her phone, and just constantly in her head - she is the last one to be approached by a man.

Forget the restaurant, forget the flirting, forget the mind-blowing sex. There is no room for all that. There is no polarity in all that.

You need to make yourself available in your body language, in your energy, and in your head.

And yes, you can't make yourself smile, but with all the principles and practices we’ve covered, you're not in your head all the time.

You're just here and now in your feminine presence.

You really can meet great men anywhere and everywhere. So, always be practicing. And let go of the guilt. Be playful. Be flirtatious. Allow yourself to be courted and have fun!

Unfollow and unscroll all the “patriarchy/toxic masculinity/feminism” blogs and influencers. Let that shit go! Get rid of everyone and everything who tells you and complains about men being bad, men not doing their part and men being toxic.

Start seeing the good in men and start being open to that good (again, Part 2 is where you learn how to do all that).

So that's how it works. You get exposed to potential suitors, then you choose.

What happens after?

We’ll get to that in the masterclass on Monday (register here).

Until then, here’s your homework. I want you to ask yourself the following questions:

If you were to imagine your ideal man, what would he be into, what kinds of places would he hang out in, how would you meet one another?

What kind of women is he interested in? How well do you match that description?

What are you currently doing to maximize your chances of meeting this man? Or are you just leaving things to chance?

Reply to this email to share your thoughts and ask me any questions you have relating to the dating funnel.

Alright, girlfriends that’s it from me for now. Looking forward to seeing you sooon!

~ Anna



P.S: We’ve been getting such great feedback from the women who are following along in this series, and are already showing up for themselves by doing the work as they go.

I hope to see you all on Monday so we can address your questions in more detail and really just solidify these principles and these tools that have totally changed my life and the life of so many of the amazing women I’m privileged to work with. So don't be shy... reply!

I'm so happy to be receiving emails like this one from Elisa, who gave us permission to share her A-ha moment after reading Part 3:
